svg.title: JuraRegel TOGAF Enterprise Architecture Landscape
svg.desc: High-resolution landscape architecture overview showing source authorities flowing into RegelSpraak, JREM, CI/CD gates, Rule APIs, and consumer channels.
svg.text: Legal RuleOps platform: source authorities to RegelSpraak, JREM, assurance gates, Rule APIs, and adoption channels
Authoritative legal, security, architecture and interoperability sources
Rule engineering pipeline, canonical exchange model, assurance, runtime APIs
Developer, architect, compliance, CI and self-hosting interfaces
API Design Rules, OAuth, events, Digikoppeling
NORA principes as enterprise architecture meta-layer
TLS, webapp security, basic principles, duty-of-care controls
Every rule keeps sourceRef, version, validity, evidence and domain owner.
npx juraregel init, check, serve, validate
executive overview, matrix, live health checks
quality gates, schema checks, BDD, harvester health
intake portals, GRC, architecture repositories, ENSIA
Consumers receive deterministic outcome, reasoning steps,
version, maturity level and legal assurance status.
Business, data, application and technology views share one assurance model: traceability, acceptance, auditability and rollback.
Stores: rule exports, JKB index, SQLite FTS5,
Domain Rule APIs, API factory, TypeScript SDK,
Integration: HTTP JSON, OpenAPI, stdio MCP,
GitHub Pages, localhost runtime, OpenAPI YAML.
Operational hooks: health endpoints, CI scripts,
threat model, SBOM, OAuth2 for production.
source validity windows and audit replay.
Legal process: griffierecht 8490, procesreglement 8491, classificatie 8492, publicatie 8493
Government compliance: BIO2 8494, Forum 8495, Overheidsstandaarden 8496, NORA 8497
Regulation and security: EU AI Act 8498, AVG/GDPR 8499, NCSC 8500, plus emerging L0 domains
Bronnen feed RegelSpraak CNL; RegelSpraak produces JREM; JREM gates CI/CD;
CI/CD releases FastAPI Rule APIs; APIs feed SDK, CLI, dashboard, playground and Docker;
CI/CD also feeds GitHub Actions for repeatable quality proof.
Source reference -> rule version -> maturity level -> validation gate -> runtime decision -> audit evidence.
This is the enterprise control plane: visible enough for architects, strict enough for CI,
and simple enough for developers to run locally.
Legal rules remain explainable artifacts: jurist-readable source, machine-validated JREM, auditable API decisions, and traceable consumer outcomes.
